markdown:
#  Assign and Send an RFQ to a Collaborator 
##  Background 
When a [change event](https://v2.support.procore.com/glossary-of-terms) occurs on your construction project, you can create an RFQ and you have the option to send it to the appropriate project collaborator for a price quote by email. If you choose to send RFQs to your collaborators, you will need to ensure that all the Prerequisites below have been met.
#####  Important
If you don't want to grant your collaborators the permissions they need to submit a quote in Procore, you also have the option of managing the submission process outside of Procore. If you choose this option, you will follow the steps in [Respond to an RFQ on Behalf of an RFQ Recipient](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/respond-to-an-rfq-on-behalf-of-a-collaborator) instead of using the steps below.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * 'Admin' level permissions on the project's Change Events tool  
AND
    * 'Admin' level permissions on the project's Commitments tool.
  * **Additional Information:**
    * If RFQs are disabled on your project, contact your Procore point of contact to request to enable them.


##  Prerequisites 
For a collaborator's name to appear as a selection in the **Assignee** drop-down list on an RFQ:
  * The collaborator's company must have a company record in the Project Directory. See [Add a Company to the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/set-up-a-project-directory/create-companies).
  * The collaborator must have an active Procore user account in the Project Directory. See [Add a User Account to the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-project/tutorials/add-user-account-to-project-directory).
  * The collaborator's user account must be granted 'Standard' level permissions on the project's Commitments tool. See [Change a User's Permissions in the Project Directory](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/directory-project/tutorials/change-a-users-permissions-in-the-project-directory).
  * The collaborator must be listed as an employee of the 'Contract Company' on the commitment and a member of the 'Private' drop-down list on the commitment. See [Create a Purchase Order](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/create-a-purchase-order) or [Create a Subcontract](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/commitments-project/tutorials/create-a-subcontract).


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Change Events** tool.
  2. Click the **RFQs** tab.
  3. Locate the RFQ in the list. Then click **View**.
  4. If the RFQ has been created, this banner appears at the top of the page.   
  
![Rfqbanner](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/7c387bbcbd70594dfed48362f8f67319/9e070a4cd08590f2a700dee0c3bc0709/rfqbanner.png?fm=webp)
  5. Click the **Add an Assignee** link.
  6. In the 'General' tab of the RFQ, select an **Assignee** from the drop-down list. 
#####  Note
If the person you are looking for is not on the list, see [Why am I unable to assign users to a RFQ?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-why-am-unable-to-assign-users-to-a-rfq)
  7. Click the **Save & Send RFQ** button.  
A 'Send RFQs' message appears.
  8. Click **Continue** to acknowledge that you want to send the RFQ email to the assignee you designated.   
A YELLOW banner appears to confirm the email communication was created. Procore sends an RFQ email to the person listed in the Assignee list.


##  Next Steps 
  * [Respond to an RFQ as a Collaborator](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/submit-a-quote-as-a-collaborator)


## See Also
  * [Respond to an RFQ on Behalf of a Collaborator](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/respond-to-an-rfq-on-behalf-of-a-collaborator)
  * [Submit a Quote as a Collaborator](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/submit-a-quote-as-a-collaborator)
  * [Create RFQs from a Change Event](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/change-events-project/tutorials/create-rfqs-from-a-change-event)
title: Assign and Send an RFQ to a Collaborator
description: How to assign and send an RFQ email notification to a project collaborator.
